event: Botanical sketch of a cemetery corner in St. Petersburg
symbols: overgrown plants on graves — nature reclaiming the domain of death, the persistence of life
description: A pencil and sepia sketch depicting a corner of a cemetery with lush vegetation growing over and around a low stone grave enclosure. Large broad-leaved plants (possibly burdock) dominate the center, surrounded by tall grasses, reeds, and bare twigs. The drawing is detailed and botanical in character, capturing each leaf and stem with precision. A Cyrillic inscription at the bottom identifies the subject and dates the work to 1840. Faint architectural elements are suggested in the background.

_meta.title: A nook of Smolensk cemetery in St. Petersburg
